findings.detail: 6 nodes across two subgraphs in a flowchart TB layout. LAYERS subgraph: USER (blue/primary) → IFACE (purple/secondary) → INTERN (dashed/hidden), with edge labels 'clean surface ↓' and 'detail hidden ↓' capturing the directional visibility model from §5.2. DECOMP subgraph: ROOT (green/done) → SUBA + SUBB, forming a minimal 3-node contrast tree matching §5.4. Governing concept 'Abstraction — hiding complexity at the right level' in config title field per title-discipline rule. Subgraph titles 'Abstraction: hides detail' and 'Decomposition: breaks apart' provide the explicit contrast the diagram intent requires. All labels ≤ 3 words, plain English. Color palette: primary:#a5d8ff (user-visible top layer), secondary:#d0bfff (interface/bridge layer), hidden:dashed #F0F0F0 (internal complexity), done:#b2f2bb (decomposition nodes as contrast panel). Dashed border on INTERN visually communicates 'hidden'. No concept drift, no scope creep, no acronyms.
